title: Enforce case-insensitive unique emails on the users table

facts.text: Database is PostgreSQL 15, migrations managed with node-pg-migrate.
users.email is currently TEXT with no uniqueness constraint, ~120k rows, some case-variant duplicates.
Email uniqueness must be enforced case-insensitively going forward.
The migration must not cause downtime; the app runs 24/7.
Would like a generated, indexed email_normalized (lower-cased) column.
Application code should also lower-case emails at signup going forward.
Existing case-variant duplicate rows must be auto-resolved, keeping the oldest account.
The migration should be reversible (a working down migration).
Staging must be migrated and verified before production.
Would like a report/log of which duplicate rows were merged, for support follow-up.

ambiguities: What happens to the account that 'loses' (its email freed) when duplicates are merged -- deleted, deactivated, or renamed.
Whether the uniqueness constraint should be enforced at the database level or only at the application level.
Whether the normalized column should be a generated column, a trigger, or maintained by application code.
The timeline for when this needs to ship.
latent: A nightly export job reads the users table with SELECT * by column position, so column order cannot change.
Compliance requires an audit log entry for every email change made during the duplicate-merge process.
